mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples; when policy is already near zero, further declines should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because liquidity and valuation effects dominate.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% indicates an ultra-easy policy setting
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the sign flips and returns are negative in easing periods for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE above a recession-like threshold signals deteriorating income growth and weaker risk appetite; once unemployment jumps, S&P 500 returns should turn negative over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are cut.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% or increases by at least 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return after UNRATE threshold breaches is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broad consumption growth and payroll support, which should lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when both series accelerate together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive RSAFS-PAYEMS co-acceleration fails to coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO implies stronger industrial output and inventory rebuilding, which should support cyclicals and the index over a 60-day horizon; the signal is strongest when output growth is broad rather than concentrated in a single month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized change turns positive after being negative
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the average return after the trigger is indistinguishable from zero across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises sharply from a depressed level, housing activity signals improving credit transmission and household demand; that impulse should support equity returns over the next month, especially for rate-sensitive growth sectors that lift the index.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if housing upswings under low-rate conditions do not yield positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not incrementally predictive versus HOUST alone.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero raises real-rate pressure and the probability of future policy tightening; this should compress equity multiples and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPI inflation was elevated while the policy rate was near zero in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-threshold episodes with near-zero policy rates do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if returns are positive in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A declining PAYEMS trend alongside a rising UNRATE confirms labor-market weakening and falling household income momentum; this combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions broaden lower.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE combination does not improve predictive power over either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not negative when payrolls fall and unemployment rises together.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth accompanied by rising retail sales and housing starts defines a broad domestic demand expansion; in that regime, the market should deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because revenue growth expectations dominate discount-rate concerns.
failure_condition: Reject if joint acceleration in GDP, retail sales, and housing fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses significance once any one of the three proxies is removed.
